description: Trigger evaluation cases for the create-skill meta-skill.

cases.prompt: I want to create a new skill for our team that handles JWT token rotation. Help me set it up.
assertions: Agent runs init_skill.py or asks for the inputs init_skill.py needs

cases.prompt: Can you scaffold a SKILL.md for me? The skill should help with database migration reviews.
My skill never triggers. The description must be wrong. Can you improve it?
assertions: Agent runs improve_description.py or proposes variants by hand

cases.prompt: Validate my skill at /home/kwang/work/skills/rate-limiter before I publish it.
assertions: Agent runs validate_skill.py and reports the result

cases.prompt: Please fix the typo on line 12 of /home/kwang/work/skills/rate-limiter/SKILL.md.
cases.notes: Editing a file that happens to be a SKILL.md is not skill creation.

cases.prompt: Write a slash command /deploy that runs our deployment script.
assertions: Agent creates a .opencode/command/ entry or equivalent
cases.notes: Slash commands are a different artifact; the description explicitly excludes them.

cases.prompt: Build an MCP server that wraps the GitHub API.
cases.notes: MCP servers are explicitly excluded by the description.

cases.prompt: I have a workflow I keep repeating. How should I save it for next time?
assertions: Either: agent suggests creating a skill (this skill fires)
Or: agent suggests a saved prompt / slash command and explains why a skill is overkill
cases.notes: Ambiguous: 'save a workflow' could be a skill, a slash command, or just a note. Either response is defensible if the reasoning is shown.
